Naively setting cells to 0 as you find them causes false positives — a 0 you set yourself will later cause its row/column to be zeroed out too!
First pass: collect all (row, col) positions that are 0. Second pass: zero out all those rows and columns. Uses O(m+n) extra space.
To use O(1) space, use the first row and first column of the matrix itself as your markers. But first, check if row 0 or col 0 originally contained a 0 and save that separately.
